experiments.description: baseline: run unmodified code to establish baseline metrics
mlp: try GELU activation instead of ReLU

idea_queue: mlp: increase hidden dims (256→512→1024→2048→4096→900)
mlp: reduce to 3 layers (16→512→1024→900) for faster training
mlp: try LeakyReLU instead of ReLU
mlp: try SiLU/Swish activation
mlp: add skip connections (input concatenated to later layers)
mlp: remove dropout entirely
mlp: increase dropout to 0.5
mlp: try layer normalization instead of batch normalization
mlp: add weight decay (1e-4) to Adam
mlp: increase learning rate to 3e-3
mlp: decrease learning rate to 1e-4
mlp: use cosine annealing scheduler instead of ReduceLROnPlateau
mlp: use OneCycleLR scheduler
mlp: add residual connections between hidden layers
mlp: use Huber loss instead of MSE
mlp: add L1 regularization for sparsity
cfp: increase residual blocks from 4 to 6
cfp: reduce residual blocks from 4 to 2
cfp: increase channel width (32→64 in residual path)
cfp: use 3x3 conv instead of 1x1 for channel projection
cfp: add batch normalization after merge
cfp: try SGD with cosine annealing LR
cfp: switch from SGD to Adam optimizer
cfp: add early stopping with patience=20
cfp: try Huber loss instead of MSE
both: reduce total batch size to 128
both: increase total batch size to 512
both: use mixed precision training (autocast)
mlp: try wider bottleneck (16→1024→2048→1024→900)
ideas_tried: baseline: run unmodified code to establish baseline metrics
mlp: reduce dropout from 0.3 to 0.1 — WORSE (RMSE 0.0540, SSIM 0.6028)
mlp: try GELU activation — WORSE (RMSE 0.0562, SSIM 0.6049)
notes: Baseline MLP RMSE 0.0534, CFP RMSE 0.0531. Both dropout reduction and GELU hurt. ReLU+BN+dropout(0.3) seems well-tuned for activations. Try training/optimizer changes next.
